description: Lock Shamanism & Animism to archaeologically inferred Natufian ritual-specialist practice, not a universal first religion or a formal 19th-century animism theory.

checks.reason: The scoped claim is anchored to the Hilazon Tachtit shaman-burial evidence dated to about 12,000 cal BP, converted to an approximate BCE by-date.
Oral transmission supports ritual knowledge, songs, stories, and roles across generations without narrowing the node to written doctrine.
Kin-based band organization is only broad social context for the Hilazon Tachtit evidence, and the local tribalism node is not a direct chronological prerequisite.
